title: Level 844
wikitext:
{{Fix|fix visual editor look (you will understand if you look into it)|date=7/2/26}}{{Rewrite|I'd say this is a pretty good page in the older standards, however it really suffers from informal and somewhat inconsistent tone, and lacks an original and interesting overall concept. There's is one recurring mistake: replace "tho", it is an informal abbreviation of "though". Make sure to read other pages to get the example of formal/clinical tone. Continuing on, the entity and the Other Side are extremely underdeveloped; I understand that you want them to be mysterious, but you can expand on that lil concept with other related stuffs. The events could be used for expansion too. As an bonus note: if you want feedback or constructive critisms for your draft, you should just directly request one of the people on the [[Backrooms Wiki:Crit Team|Crit Team]] via their message walls, so it would be much more harder for them to just ignore your request and say somebody else will do the job. |date=1, July, 2026}} {{Class 2}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 844: "Park Path Walk"}}
[[File:{{{file|Park Background.jpeg}}}|link=]]
[[File:{{{file|Park Road.png}}}|{{{width|300px}}}|{{{hovertext| }}}|alt={{{alt| }}}|link=]]
{{{caption|An image of the main path of Level 844.}}}
[[File:{{{file|Park Road.png}}}|{{{bg-size|600px}}}|link=]]
__NOTOC__ '''Level 844''' is a seemingly peaceful park consisting of an endless asphalt path. == '''Description''' == Level 844 strongly resembles a well-maintained suburban park from the Frontrooms on a bright summer day. A wide asphalt path runs through the level, bordered by tall grass, colorful wildflowers, scattered trees, and occasional wooden benches and trash bins. The sky is a consistent bright blue with slow-moving white clouds. The terrain has a lot of hills. The level follows a normal 24-hour day/night cycle. During daytime the sun shines brightly, while at night the area becomes darker with thousands of fireflies glowing softly antique white color in the grass and trees, creating an eerily beautiful sight. Some wanderers report seing bright red fireflys too but this is not confirmed. The Nights on this level are really bright though and wanderers can see almost as good as during the day time. No matter what time it is tho, the level is really relaxing and restores sanity fast. Wanderers in Level 844 experience a strong mental compulsion to remain on the asphalt path. Taking 3 or more steps off the path into the grass causes increasing headaches and an overwhelming urge to return. The intensity of the pain grows with distance and time spent off the path. Drones and other mechanisms also can not leave the path, since they will burn down from the inside if they try to. Although the path appears to stretch infinitely into the distance, it is actually a very large closed loop. Most wanderers fail to notice they are walking in circles tho due to a calming effect the level produces. This same effect reduces caution, making wanderers less likely to question anomalies such as the looping path or frequent movement seen in their peripheral vision. Even after leaving the level this effect stays for around an hour. The wooden benches on Level 844 that wanderers sometimes come across actually restore their stamina really fast when they sit on them so wanderers can start walking again faster and not waste much time sitting. The trash bins are regular trash bins from the Frontrooms though and they will always be empty. If a wanderer puts a item inside of the trash bin the item will disapear immediately after they look away. It is unknown where the item vanishes to. It is impossible for two or more wanderers to perceive, hear, or interact with each other inside Level 844, even if they entered together. Wanderers will simply not see other humans. == '''Entities''' == A single entity has been consistently reported in Level 844. Wanderers describe a small, light-gray humanoid figure resembling a young girl with what looks like cat ears, usually observed standing motionless among the trees or tall grass. This entity has been nicknamed '''"Lindy"''' by the wanderers, but this entity doesn't have an official name yet. If a wanderer makes direct eye contact with Lindy, they will immediately suffer a severe headache similar to the effect of leaving the path, after which the entity disappears. Sightings are rare and inconsistent. Many wanderers believe Lindy is more of a hallucination or local myth than an actual entity. Her nature and purpose (if any) remain unknown. == '''Colonies and Outposts''' == Due to the isolation effect of the level no colonies or outposts can be established inside of Level 844. == '''Events''' == Rain occurs very rarely in Level 844, typically once every one to two weeks. During these events, the previously clear sky turns dark gray, and lightning can occasionally be seen striking in the distance. The rainwater is normal potable water and is safe for consumption. During rainfall, '''Lindy''' has frequently been reported standing directly on the asphalt path. This is currently the only known situation in which eye contact with the entity does not cause a severe headache. However, if Lindy notices a wanderer looking at her, she will quickly flee into the surrounding grass and vanish. Attempting to ignite any object or start a fire in Level 844 will immediately trigger a heavy rainstorm. Shortly after the rain begins, the wanderer will be forcibly expelled from the level. == '''Entrances and Exits''' == === '''Entrances:''' === # Interacting with tall grass in any Backrooms level has a really small chance of transporting the wanderer to Level 844. # Noclipping into grass or flowerbeds in certain outdoor-themed levels may also lead here. === '''Exits:''' === # After remaining in Level 844 for a prolonged period, distant rustling and whispering noises begin emanating from the tall grass. Interacting with the grass while these noises are present will return the wanderer to the level they originally entered from. # Igniting a fire on Level 844 will result in a heavy rainstorm starting and the wanderer who caused the fire will be kicked from Level 844 to the level they originally entered from. == '''Addendum''' == > "At first it was peaceful. The fireflies at night were honestly beautiful. But after walking for what felt like days, I realized the same bench kept appearing... and that little gray girl in the trees kept watching. I got out as soon as I heard the whispers that called my name." > — Unknown Wanderer'' > ''"You know, I actually liked that level when I first discovered it. Nice blue skys with white clouds at day and relaxing atmosphere at night. But... that girl... I don't know why but I just didn't care about her. I was like even though it's not! Imagine if that thing was hostile and dangerous! I would not be standing here right now." > — Unknown Wanderer'' == '''The Other Side''' ==
'''The Burned side:''' [[File:Forest Fire.jpeg|alt=An image of the '''Burning side'''.|thumb|An image of the '''Burning side'''.]] During a rain event in Level 844, lightning will extremely rarely strike the asphalt path. This leaves a small charred, smoking spot for roughly 5–10 seconds. If a wanderer touches this spot before the rain washes it away, they will be transported to the "'''Bruned side'''" — a flipped and destroyed version of the level 844. Due to the effect of this part of the Level and the difficult entrance not much is known about it. == '''Description''' == On this alternate side, gravity is inverted so the wanderer remains standing on the asphalt path. The sky is a dark, oppressive red, constantly raining ash. Everything surrounding the path is burned to ash and engulfed in flames. The air is thick with smoke and is highly unpleasant to breathe. Unlike the main level, stepping off the path on the '''Burned side''' does not cause a headache. However, the surrounding area is actively burning, making leaving the path extremely dangerous. Wanderers experience a constant, painful headache that grows steadily worse the longer they remain on the '''Burned side'''. The usual calming effect of Level 844 is completely absent here. The benches are all burned down on the '''Burned side''' and do not restore the wanderers stamina anymore. It is also theorized that the trash bins connect to both sides and if a wanderer puts an item inside a trash bin it will be transported to the other side of the level. But this is just a theory. == '''Entities''' == The entity ('''Lindy''') is significantly more hostile on the '''Burned side'''. It frequently appears directly in front of the wanderer, often leaving no time to look away. This causes an immediate and severe headache. == '''Exits''' == *The wanderer can return to the main Level 844 at any time by clearly, loudly vocalizing a desire to leave (e.g. “I want to leave”, “Let me out”, or similar phrases). Doing so results in immediate teleportation back to the original level. == '''Addendum''' == > ''"This was awful to be there. Everything was so hot and in flames. It's funny how you can't egnite anything on the main side of the Level but the other side is just... This. Maybe this is why the Level hates fire so much?" > — Anonymous Wanderer''
== '''Update on level 14.5.2026''' ==
[[File:{{{file|Park Lake 2.png}}}|{{{width|300px}}}|{{{hovertext| }}}|alt={{{alt| }}}|link=]]
{{{caption|The image of the lake and shore that are located beyond the path of level 844.}}}
[[File:{{{file|Park Lake 2.png}}}|{{{bg-size|600px}}}|link=]]
On May 14th, 2026, a new discovery was made regarding Level 844: If a wanderer brings any toy from '''[[Level 18]]''' and leaves it on a bench in Level 844, then completes one full loop of the path, the toy will disappear upon return. This action temporarily nullifies the path compulsion, allowing the wanderer to leave the asphalt path for approximately 15 minutes. After 15 minutes have elapsed, the wanderer is automatically expelled from Level 844 and deposited in a playground area within '''[[Level 613]]'''. During this window, the discovering wanderer reported locating a small lake a short distance from the path, with a blue stand-up paddleboard (SUP) resting on the shore. Upon attempting to use the paddleboard, the 15-minute period ended, resulting in immediate expulsion to Level 613. The exact distance of the lake from the main path, as well as its properties and possible exits, remain unknown. The wanderer responsible for this discovery has requested to remain anonymous. They have declined to provide further details regarding their exploration but have submitted a photograph of the lake area to the research team. {{Redirect|Prev=843|Next=845}}
